q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: HttpResponseRedirect(path) only constructs an HTTP response object; it performs no network I/O, shell execution, or external side effects in the test process.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The open redirect violation is encoded in the returned HttpResponseRedirect object, specifically its Location header set from the tainted path variable.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and returns.exprs includes 'response', which is the HttpResponseRedirect(path) object containing the tainted redirect URL.
